title: Neural Networks: Zero to Hero
description: Andrej Karpathy's ground-up video series that builds neural networks from scratch in Python, culminating in a from-scratch GPT. Covers backpropagation, MLP, WaveNet, and the transformer architecture. Essential foundation for anyone who wants to truly understand how LLMs work rather than just use them.

title: A Hackers Guide to Language Models
description: Practical walkthrough by Jeremy Howard covering how LLMs work, tokenization, fine-tuning, and how to use them effectively in real applications. Aimed at practitioners who want intuition without heavy math. Great for getting up to speed quickly on capabilities and limitations.

title: RAG From Scratch — LangChain Tutorial Series
description: Twelve-part video series from LangChain covering retrieval-augmented generation from first principles. Goes from basic indexing and retrieval through advanced techniques like query transformation, reranking, and HyDE. Each video has accompanying code in a runnable notebook.

title: Building RAG Applications with minsearch and OpenAI
description: Hands-on workshop repo that builds a complete RAG pipeline using minsearch for in-memory search and the OpenAI API. Covers document ingestion, indexing, prompt construction, and the search-prompt-llm function pattern. The fastest way to get a working RAG system without heavyweight dependencies.

title: Advanced RAG Techniques: Reranking, HyDE, and Multi-Query
description: Illustrated overview of techniques that improve retrieval quality beyond naive top-k search: hypothetical document embeddings (HyDE), query decomposition, cross-encoder reranking, and parent-child chunking. Explains when to apply each technique and the tradeoffs in latency vs accuracy.

title: Chunking Strategies for RAG: What Actually Works
description: Practical guide comparing fixed-size, recursive, semantic, and document-aware chunking strategies. Includes benchmarks showing how chunk size and overlap affect retrieval precision. Covers overlap windows, sliding window chunking, and how to choose the right strategy for different document types.

title: Prompt Engineering Guide
description: OpenAI's official guide to writing effective prompts. Covers six core strategies: writing clear instructions, splitting complex tasks, using reference text, giving the model time to think, using external tools, and testing changes systematically. Includes worked examples for each technique.

title: Advanced Prompt Engineering: Chain of Thought and Self-Consistency
description: Deep dive into reasoning-based prompting techniques including chain-of-thought, zero-shot CoT, self-consistency, and tree-of-thought. Covers the research behind each technique with practical examples. Useful for building LLM applications that need reliable multi-step reasoning.

title: Prompt Injection Attacks and Defenses
description: Simon Willison's analysis of prompt injection risks in LLM applications and practical defense patterns including the dual-LLM pattern. Essential reading before deploying any AI system that processes user-provided input alongside trusted instructions.

title: Text Embeddings Explained Visually
description: Jay Alammar's visual explainer of word and sentence embeddings, from Word2Vec through modern sentence transformers. Covers cosine similarity, embedding spaces, and why embeddings are the backbone of semantic search and RAG. Excellent mental model builder with no code required.

title: Sentence Transformers: Building Semantic Search
description: Official sentence-transformers tutorial covering how to compute sentence embeddings, measure semantic similarity, and build a semantic search system. Includes practical code for bi-encoder retrieval and cross-encoder reranking, which is the two-stage retrieval pattern used in production RAG systems.

title: Vector Databases: A Beginner's Guide
description: Pinecone's introduction to vector databases explaining how ANN (approximate nearest neighbor) search works, the difference between flat and HNSW indexes, and when to use a dedicated vector store vs a relational database with pgvector. Includes a comparison of Pinecone, Weaviate, Qdrant, and Chroma.

title: Qdrant Quickstart: Vectors, Collections, and Search
description: Hands-on Qdrant tutorial that walks through creating a collection, upserting vectors with payloads, and running filtered semantic search. Qdrant is open-source and easy to run locally, making it the right choice for prototyping before committing to a hosted vector database.

title: Fine-tuning LLMs with LoRA and QLoRA
description: Practical walkthrough of parameter-efficient fine-tuning using LoRA (Low-Rank Adaptation) and QLoRA with Hugging Face PEFT. Shows how to fine-tune a 7B model on a single GPU. Covers dataset preparation, training loop, and evaluating a fine-tuned model vs the base.

title: Fine-tuning vs RAG: When to Use Each
description: Anyscale's analysis of when fine-tuning adds value vs RAG. The core insight: fine-tuning improves style, format, and behavior but doesn't reliably inject new factual knowledge — RAG is better for keeping knowledge current. Includes a decision framework for choosing between the two approaches.

title: Building AI Agents with OpenAI Function Calling
description: OpenAI's guide to function calling, which is the foundation of tool-using agents. Covers how to define tool schemas, how the model decides when to call a tool, and how to handle multi-turn conversations with tool results. The prerequisite for building any agent that can take actions.

title: ReAct: Synergizing Reasoning and Acting in LLMs
description: The original ReAct paper and demo showing how interleaving reasoning traces with action steps dramatically improves agent performance on multi-step tasks. The ReAct pattern (Thought → Action → Observation loop) is the foundation of most modern agentic frameworks including LangChain Agents.

title: Multi-Agent Systems with AutoGen
description: Introduction to Microsoft AutoGen for building multi-agent workflows where specialized agents collaborate to solve complex tasks. Covers agent roles, message passing, human-in-the-loop patterns, and how to debug agent conversations. Good mental model for understanding agentic architectures.

title: Evaluating LLM Applications: A Practical Framework
description: Hamel Husain's guide to building evaluation pipelines for LLM applications. Covers unit tests for deterministic behavior, LLM-as-a-judge for open-ended outputs, and human evaluation workflows. Argues that evals are the highest-leverage investment in LLM app development and shows how to build them cheaply.

title: RAGAS: Evaluation Framework for RAG Pipelines
description: RAGAS quickstart showing how to automatically generate a test set from your documents and evaluate a RAG pipeline on faithfulness, answer relevancy, context precision, and context recall. The fastest path to a quantitative baseline for a RAG system.

title: Deploying LLM Apps with FastAPI and Docker
description: Guide to wrapping an LLM pipeline in a production-ready FastAPI service, including request validation with Pydantic, async endpoints for concurrent inference, streaming responses, and containerizing with Docker. The standard pattern for shipping an AI backend to production.

title: LLMOps: Monitoring and Observability in Production
description: Overview of LLM observability using MLflow Tracing: how to log traces, measure latency per step, track token costs, and debug retrieval failures in production RAG systems. Covers the difference between logging, tracing, and evaluation and how they fit together in an LLMOps workflow.

title: Full Stack LLM Bootcamp — Lecture Videos
description: Free lecture series from the Full Stack Deep Learning team covering the entire LLM application stack: prompting, LLMOps, UX for AI, and deployment. Particularly strong on the product and engineering tradeoffs that matter when shipping real LLM applications, not just research demos.

title: Python for Data Science: NumPy, Pandas, and Matplotlib
description: Wes McKinney's Python for Data Analysis textbook covering NumPy arrays, Pandas DataFrames, and Matplotlib visualization. Essential reference for anyone working with structured data in Python. Free to read online. Focus on chapters 4 (NumPy), 5-8 (Pandas), and 9 (plotting).

title: Async Python for LLM Applications
description: Real Python's deep dive into asyncio, covering coroutines, event loops, and async context managers. Essential for building LLM applications that need to make concurrent API calls — for example, evaluating 100 RAG responses in parallel instead of sequentially. Includes practical examples with httpx and OpenAI's async client.
